title: First Solar Q4 2025 Earnings Summary
text: First Solar delivered strong results for Q4 2025 and full year 2025 with record module sales of 17.5 gigawatts sold, representing a 24% year-over-year increase. Net sales reached $5.2 billion, at the upper end of guidance, with diluted EPS of $14.21 per share, within guidance range. Year-end cash totaled $2.9 billion gross cash, $2.4 billion net cash. Full-year gross margin declined to 41% from 44%, primarily due to tariff costs and facility underutilization, international production constraints particularly in Southeast Asia, and high warehousing expenses of $200 million in 2025. These headwinds were partially offset by $1.6 billion in Section 45X tax credits. 2026 Outlook: Management projects net sales guidance of $4.9–$5.2 billion, gross margin approximately 49.5% including Section 45X credits, adjusted EBITDA of $2.6–$2.8 billion, U.S. production of 13.0–13.3 gigawatts, and global production of 16.5–17.5 gigawatts. Strategic developments include CURE conversion beginning at Ohio facility in Q1 2026, perovskite pilot line expected operational in early 2027, and Oxford PV licensing agreement for crystalline-silicon-free perovskite modules. The company maintains 50.1 gigawatt contracted backlog valued at $15 billion with U.S. bookings at $0.364 per watt including CURE adders. Southeast Asia facilities intentionally operating at approximately 20% utilization as strategic option.
